title: Three Visions of Green AI — And Why the Differences Matter
content: Green AI will likely continue to stretch as different communities bring their own priorities and imaginaries to the term. But if its dominant forms remain tethered to extractive assumptions, it risks becoming little more than an alibi for the systems driving planetary breakdown.

creator: Sharon Stein
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: The Magic of Feedback
content: The entire Community of Life—humans included—were better off when shrouded in the mysterious magic of the living world: held in awe, humility, and respect. We came into being inside the feedback loop, and threaten to destroy much when presuming to extract ourselves from its magical protection.

creator: Tom Murphy

title: Crazy Town. Episode 121: Being Team Human in Crazy Town
content: The question of who’s the real nut often arises for us collapse-aware folks living here in Crazy Town. Since Mr. Peanut is no longer returning their phone calls, Rob, Jason, and Asher invite Douglas Rushkoff, media theorist, professor, and host of the Team Human Podcast to answer the question.

title: What to Do as the World Falls Apart: A Framework for Action
content: This week’s Frankly marks a turning point in the work of The Great Simplification. Having spent twenty years articulating the more-than-human predicament, Nate shifts from diagnosis to direction as current events – including conflict in the Strait of Hormuz – accelerate the timeline. Today Nate shares a first-pass framework for action and response that’s organized around what to do now, which could be applied to various places and at multiple scales.

creator: Nate Hagens
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Foragers and farmers: further thoughts on a debate with Tom Murphy
content: As I’ve emphasized repeatedly here, the fundamental problem isn’t the contextual distinction between farming and foraging. It’s the way that predatory states exploit both. But now we need to find more resilient, local, stress-tolerant strategies.

creator: Chris Smaje

title: Essay Five – Facing the Abyss
content: Hunter-gatherers engaged fully in life with their psyches intact. They experienced far richer meaning and deeper relational being than we can now even imagine. That is what we must recover as we fight back.

creator: Kevin R. Nelson

title: Entropia and the Disintegration of Empire
content: As the current situation (March 2026) around the Strait of Hormuz continues to destabilise the global economy, it is timely to return to Alexander’s analysis outlining ‘the Disintegration of Empire’ (being Chapter Two of Entropia).

title: Is the complacency in global financial markets warranted?
content: Financial markets so far have reflected the belief that the Iran war will be over soon and that commerce will return to normal. I explain why I think this belief is unwarranted and why President Trump's latest announcement regarding bombing Iran's power plants is economic suicide.

creator: Kurt Cobb

title: Worse than 2008?
content: Several commentators have remarked that the United States’ war on Iran carries echoes of 2008. A potential financial crash this year could actually be much worse.
snippet: Several commentators have remarked that the United States’ war on Iran carries echoes of 2008. A potential financial crash this year could actually be much worse.

title: Iran to Trump: If you destroy us, you destroy yourself
content: We are now witnessing a game a civilization-widethe other to blink. The longer this goes on, the worse the damage for the entirety of global society. My post for this week: chicken in the Persian Gulf region.

title: Sustainable gardening in the real world
content: There are many answers, and maybe none are completely right. But some of them are better than others. You find something that works for you and your land because you kept working at it. That is what most advice leaves out, and that is where the real work is.

creator: Ciarán De Buitléar

title: Inside the Off-Grid Earthship Community in New Mexico (YouTube film review)
content: Mainstream coverage of off-grid, self-sustaining communities like the one featured in this video tends to be glib and sensational (focusing, for example, on “trash homes”). It’s so much rarer to see in-depth coverage of the full social, technical and ecological aspects of such communities, or intimate glimpses into residents’ daily lives and motivations.

creator: Frank Kaminski

title: Finding Life in the Flux
content: Since stories serve in every culture as the workshops of meaning, the urge to craft new ones may signal our readiness at long last to face up to what’s coming. All stories have characters. The qualities we attach to the ones in leading roles and the fates that befall them as plots unfold tell us a great deal about what we fear and what we value.

creator: Brian Lloyd
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Burning Questions: Where’s the Leverage to Address Compounding Crises?
content: Leslie Davenport's new podcast, Burning Questions: Conversations About Our Living World, brings together thought leaders navigating the emotional, strategic, and relational dimensions of our planetary moment. In this episode, Leslie is joined by Dr. Elizabeth Sawin to explore something both timely and timeless: how do we find leverage for transformation when the crises keep compounding?

creator: Leslie Davenport
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Finding Home Part II: Food
content: To be clear, a sustainable farmer does not grow food. With adequate nutrition from the soil, with energy from the sun, and moisture from the rain, plants do all the growing by themselves. And animals grow by acquiring the energy and nutrition from plants.

creator: Zia Gallina

title: Yet another apocalyptic prediction…
content: From rising GDP losses to ecosystem collapse, climate reports are stacking up fast. The problem is we have no language for the difference between a bad situation and a civilisational threshold.
snippet: From rising GDP losses to ecosystem collapse, climate reports are stacking up fast. The problem is we have no language for the difference between a bad situation and a civilisational threshold.
creator: Ben Shread-Hewitt

title: Our Civilization’s Disease Has a Name: Windigo
content: The Windigo diagnosis reveals that the threat we face is not only ecological or political. It is civilizational. It is rooted in a system whose deepest logic is to convert the living world into fuel for its own endless expansion.
